name: People Driven
description_short: Michigan-based credit union serving members since 1928 with competitive rates on loans, savings accounts, and digital banking services.
description_long:
People Driven Credit Union of Michigan was founded in Detroit in 1928 during the financial crisis and has operated as a member-owned cooperative for nearly a century. The credit union serves members throughout Michigan with a focus on putting member financial well-being over profits, as stated on their website. They operate multiple physical branch locations and offer comprehensive financial services through both in-person and digital channels.

PDCU offers a broad range of banking and lending products including checking and savings accounts, money market accounts, CDs, youth accounts, and investment accounts. On the lending side, they provide personal loans, auto loans, home equity loans, RV loans, debt consolidation loans, business loans, and a quick-cash product (PD Quick Cash) for qualifying members. They also issue credit cards with rates as low as 3.99% APR. Their digital banking platform includes MyPDCU app with PDCUpay functionality for peer-to-peer money transfers.

The credit union distinguishes itself through its cooperative structure, member testimonials emphasizing friendly service across all branches, and a long operational history in Michigan. They offer a 0.25% APR discount for members who enroll in autopay, and their PD Quick Cash product provides up to $2,000 to qualifying members in minutes. The minimum deposit to become a member is just $5, and they emphasize advanced fraud protection and privacy safeguards.

As a traditional credit union, PDCU is best suited for Michigan residents seeking relationship-based banking with competitive rates and member-focused service. The primary limitation is geographic restriction to Michigan membership, and like most credit unions, they may have fewer physical locations than large national banks. Rates shown are promotional minimums and actual rates depend on creditworthiness and loan terms.
pros: Cooperative structure prioritizes member well-being over profits
Extremely low membership requirement ($5 deposit only)
Competitive loan rates starting as low as 3.99% APR for credit cards and 5.24% for auto loans
PD Quick Cash provides up to $2,000 in minutes for qualifying members
24/7 digital banking with MyPDCU app and PDCUpay peer-to-peer transfers
Long operational history (since 1928) and strong member retention testimonials
0.25% APR discount available for autopay enrollment
Comprehensive product range including personal, business, vehicle, and home equity loans
cons: Limited to Michigan membership only—geographic restriction excludes other states
Fewer physical branch locations compared to national banks
Quoted rates are promotional minimums; actual rates depend on credit profile and may be significantly higher
No mention of online account opening process or mobile-first banking experience compared to neobanks
Personal loan rates at 10.74% minimum are higher than some alternative lenders
best_for: Michigan residents seeking traditional banking with member-owned cooperative benefits
Credit union members wanting competitive rates on auto, home equity, or personal loans
Small business owners in Michigan needing business loans and checking accounts
Families wanting to build credit history and teach children financial habits through youth accounts
diagnosis: People Driven Credit Union is best for Michigan residents who value cooperative banking principles, want relationship-based service, and seek competitive rates on loans and savings products. The main caveat is strict geographic limitation to Michigan membership and promotional rates that may vary significantly based on individual creditworthiness; applicants should verify actual rates before applying.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
